item.text: I’ll inspect the finding first, then trace the relevant code path before editing so the fix stays narrow and behavior-preserving.

item.text: The finding points to two authorization gaps: task result acceptance and service monitor report ingestion. I’m reading the surrounding models and singleton state now to find the existing owner/server relationship checks to reuse.

    99		}
   100		if data, err := json.Marshal(m.FailTriggerTasks); err != nil {
   101			return err
   102		} else {
   103			m.FailTriggerTasksRaw = string(data)
   104		}
   105		if data, err := json.Marshal(m.RecoverTriggerTasks); err != nil {

   107		} else {
   108			m.RecoverTriggerTasksRaw = string(data)
   109		}
   110		return nil
   111	}

   113	func (m *Service) AfterFind(tx *gorm.DB) error {
   114		m.SkipServers = make(map[uint64]bool)
   115		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(m.SkipServersRaw), &m.SkipServers); err != nil {
   116			log.Println("NEZHA>> Service.AfterFind:", err)
   117			return nil
   118		}
   119	
   120		// 加载触发任务列表
   121		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(m.FailTriggerTasksRaw), &m.FailTriggerTasks); err != nil {
   122			return err
   123		}
   124		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(m.RecoverTriggerTasksRaw), &m.RecoverTriggerTasks); err != nil {
   125			return err
   126		}
   127	
   128		return nil
   129	}

   131	// IsServiceSentinelNeeded 判断该任务类型是否需要进行服务监控 需要则返回true
   132	func IsServiceSentinelNeeded(t uint64) bool {
   133		switch t {
   134		case TaskTypeCommand, TaskTypeTerminalGRPC, TaskTypeUpgrade,
   135			TaskTypeKeepalive, TaskTypeNAT, TaskTypeFM,
   136			TaskTypeReportConfig, TaskTypeApplyConfig:
   137			return false
   138		default:
   139			return true
   140		}
   141	}

item.text: There are existing ownership helpers for cron dispatch, but no corresponding check when results come back. I’m checking how services are dispatched so the report-side validation matches the service cover rules instead of inventing a parallel policy.

Service dispatch already limits eligible reporters by service cover plus server ownership. The remediation should enforce that same predicate at ingestion time, because task streams and client reports are separate trust boundaries.

item.text: The edit will add two receive-side guards: command results must match a recorded task dispatch to that server, and service reports must come from a server that the service could have been dispatched to. I’m keeping the public behavior of task scheduling unchanged.

The cron side now records successful sends and consumes one recorded assignment per accepted result. Next I’m applying the service report guard and updating the existing dispatch code to call the same predicate.
The service guard is in place. I still need to expose a shared owner check and replace the dashboard dispatch helper, then wire cron result validation into the RPC stream.
I’m now replacing duplicate service ownership logic in dashboard RPC with the singleton predicate so dispatch and report ingestion stay in sync.
The dashboard dispatcher now uses the shared service predicate. Next I’m changing the vulnerable cron result branch to reject reports without a matching recorded assignment.
I’m adding regression coverage for the exact trust-boundary cases: cron results without a recorded dispatch, single-use cron result acceptance, and service reports from unauthorized reporters.
The regression tests are in place. I’m formatting and running the relevant Go test packages now to catch compile issues and verify the security cases.

item.text:
Implemented the authorization remediation locally.

Changed:
- `service/rpc/nezha.go`: cron command results are only accepted if the reporting server has a recorded dispatch for that cron task.
- `service/singleton/crontask.go`: records successful cron task dispatches per server, consumes them on result acceptance, and clears stale records on cron deletion.
- `service/singleton/servicesentinel.go`: service monitor reports are rejected unless the reporter is an eligible server for that service owner and cover rule.
- `cmd/dashboard/rpc/rpc.go`: dispatch now uses the same shared service authorization predicate as report ingestion.
- `service/singleton/security_regression_test.go`: added regression tests for unauthorized cron results and service reports.

Verification:
- Ran `/usr/local/go/bin/gofmt`.
- Ran `git diff --check` successfully.
- `go test` could not complete because required modules are not cached and access to `proxy.golang.org` is blocked by the environment egress allowlist.
